acceptable_sentence: The union broke off the talks.
unacceptable_sentence: The union broke off the proposal.
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to negotiation contexts; the unacceptable item targets the fact that break off selects talks or relations, not a proposal itself.
critical_note: Use negotiation/relationship contexts; avoid object-category anomalies such as agenda.

acceptable_sentence: The guard handed over the prisoner.
unacceptable_sentence: The guard handed over the arrest.
pair_rationale: Both objects are legal-domain nouns; the unacceptable item keeps the domain but uses an event noun that cannot be handed over as custody or control.
critical_note: Use possession/control-transfer contexts with plausible entities; avoid non-transferable abstractions.

acceptable_sentence: The team moved up to first place.
unacceptable_sentence: The team moved up to last place.
pair_rationale: Both outcomes are ranking positions; rejection depends on knowing that move up denotes upward rank movement.
critical_note: Hierarchy/status contrast is locally tied to the PV and comparatively robust.

acceptable_sentence: The passengers got on the train.
unacceptable_sentence: The passengers got on the station.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are transport-domain nouns; only a vehicle-like noun is compatible with the boarding sense.
critical_note: Transport sense is clear, but unacceptable item must remain in transport context rather than object category.

acceptable_sentence: One essay stood out among submissions.
unacceptable_sentence: One essay stood out among pages.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are writing-domain nouns; the unacceptable item changes the comparison set from same-class submissions to document parts.
critical_note: Use comparison among same-class candidates; avoid odd comparison sets.

acceptable_sentence: Investors pulled back from the project.
unacceptable_sentence: Investors pulled back from the approval.
pair_rationale: Both complements are decision-domain nouns; the unacceptable item uses a state/result where an activity or commitment is expected.
critical_note: Withdrawal sense can work if both conditions describe activities or commitments.

acceptable_sentence: The lab ran out of samples.
unacceptable_sentence: The lab ran out of findings.
pair_rationale: Both objects are research-domain nouns; samples can be depleted as supplies, whereas findings are not normally used up as a stock.
critical_note: Depletion can be tested with countable resources; avoid impossible object categories.

acceptable_sentence: The clerk turned over the records to police.
unacceptable_sentence: The clerk turned over the permission to police.
pair_rationale: Both objects are administrative/legal nouns; records can be surrendered to authorities, but permission is not an object surrendered in this frame.
critical_note: Authority-transfer sense can work, but literal turning-over sense must be suppressed.

acceptable_sentence: The theater put on a musical.
unacceptable_sentence: The theater put on a ticket.
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to theater contexts; the unacceptable item uses a theater-related artifact rather than an event or performance.
critical_note: Event-staging sense can work, but clothing/application senses are strong competitors.

acceptable_sentence: The team took back the lead.
unacceptable_sentence: The team took back the loss.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are sports-outcome nouns; lead can be regained, whereas loss is not normally regained by taking it back.
critical_note: Use regain-control contexts; current acceptable sentence also needs rewriting.

acceptable_sentence: The investment paid off quickly.
unacceptable_sentence: The invoice paid off quickly.
pair_rationale: Both subjects are finance-domain nouns; investment can yield a benefit, while invoice is not normally an intransitive source of payoff.
critical_note: Outcome-benefit sense can work if effort/investment and result are controlled.

acceptable_sentence: The passengers got off the ferry.
unacceptable_sentence: The passengers got off the terminal.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are transport-domain nouns; only the vehicle-like noun supports the leave-a-vehicle sense.
critical_note: Vehicle-leaving sense can work in travel contexts; avoid broad non-vehicle objects.

acceptable_sentence: The witness turned up at court.
unacceptable_sentence: The witness turned up at testimony.
pair_rationale: Both complements are legal-domain nouns; court is a location/event site, whereas testimony is not a place where someone turns up.
critical_note: Arrival sense can work, but turn up also means increase volume and be found.

acceptable_sentence: The host cut off the caller abruptly.
unacceptable_sentence: The host cut off the topic abruptly.
pair_rationale: Both objects belong to talk-show discourse; the target sense selects an active speaker, not the topic of conversation.
critical_note: Interruption sense can work with speaking contexts; avoid object-cutting/service-stop senses.

acceptable_sentence: The firm brought in a consultant.
unacceptable_sentence: The firm brought in a consultation.
pair_rationale: Both objects are professional-service nouns; the target sense requires a person or role, not the service event itself.
critical_note: Recruit/ask-someone sense requires human role contexts; many abstractions can be brought in.

acceptable_sentence: The report set out the procedure.
unacceptable_sentence: The report set out the appendix.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are document-domain nouns; the target sense selects an explanation or rules, not a document section as such.
critical_note: Explain/present-clearly sense can work in report/policy contexts; literal laying-out/travel senses compete.

acceptable_sentence: The coach broke down the play.
unacceptable_sentence: The coach broke down the whistle.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are sports-domain nouns; a play can be analyzed into parts, whereas a whistle is not an analytical problem in this frame.
critical_note: Analysis/decomposition sense can work with problem/task contexts; avoid physical failure sense.

acceptable_sentence: The nurse reached out to patients.
unacceptable_sentence: The nurse reached out to symptoms.
pair_rationale: Both complements are health-domain nouns; the target sense requires a person or group that can be contacted.
critical_note: Communication/help sense can work with human/institutional recipients.

acceptable_sentence: The crew put up the banner.
unacceptable_sentence: The crew put up the brochure.
pair_rationale: Both objects are display/publicity materials, but brochure remains too plausible in a posting context; keep as reserve pending native review.
critical_note: Display/attach sense can work but must avoid build/tolerate/price-increase senses.

acceptable_sentence: The coach turned around the season.
unacceptable_sentence: The coach turned around the defeat.
pair_rationale: Both objects are sports-outcome nouns; a season can be transformed, whereas a defeat itself is not normally turned around after the fact.
critical_note: Improvement-after-problems sense can work with teams/companies if outcome words are controlled.

acceptable_sentence: The data backed up the conclusion.
unacceptable_sentence: The data backed up the confusion.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are research-discourse nouns; data can support a conclusion, but not confusion as a claim.
critical_note: Evidence-support sense can work; computer copy/reverse senses must be suppressed.

acceptable_sentence: She took out a mortgage.
unacceptable_sentence: She took out a receipt.
pair_rationale: Both objects are finance/document nouns; mortgage fits the obtain-an-official-service sense, while receipt does not.
critical_note: Official-service sense can work with loans/policies; remove-from-container and social-date senses compete.

acceptable_sentence: The secret came out during trial.
unacceptable_sentence: The witness came out during trial.
pair_rationale: Both subjects are legal-domain nouns; the acceptable subject is hidden information, whereas the unacceptable subject is a person and risks alternate literal readings.
critical_note: Reveal sense can work with information/truth/news contexts; avoid subject-category anomalies.

acceptable_sentence: The reforms brought about change.
unacceptable_sentence: The reforms brought about evidence.
pair_rationale: Both objects are policy/research nouns; reforms can cause change, whereas evidence is normally produced or found rather than brought about by reforms.
critical_note: Causation sense can work in academic contexts if result nouns are controlled.

acceptable_sentence: The researchers carried out the analysis.
unacceptable_sentence: The researchers carried out the hypothesis.
pair_rationale: Both objects are research-domain nouns; analysis is a task that can be carried out, whereas hypothesis is a proposition to test.
critical_note: Conduct/perform task sense is strong for academic LJT; use plausible wrong task objects.

acceptable_sentence: The doctor ruled out infection.
unacceptable_sentence: The doctor ruled out symptoms.
pair_rationale: Both objects are medical-domain nouns; infection can be excluded as a possibility, whereas symptoms are observed signs, not the ruled-out condition.
critical_note: Exclude-possibility sense is a strong academic candidate with manageable near-misses.

acceptable_sentence: The review pointed out the limitation.
unacceptable_sentence: The review pointed out the appendix.
pair_rationale: Both objects are academic-document nouns; the review can identify a limitation, whereas appendix is a document part and creates weaker target fit.
critical_note: Draw-attention sense is strong if both sentences keep human/author subjects.

acceptable_sentence: The abstract summed up the study.
unacceptable_sentence: The abstract summed up the heading.
pair_rationale: Both nouns are academic-document nouns; an abstract summarizes a study, not a heading.
critical_note: Summarize-content sense is strong if both conditions use text/content objects.

acceptable_sentence: The doctor followed up after the treatment.
unacceptable_sentence: The doctor followed up after the thermometer.
pair_rationale: Both complements are medical-domain nouns; the follow-up frame requires an event or case, not an instrument.
critical_note: Further-action sense can work with medical/research follow-up contexts.

acceptable_sentence: The diagram laid out the process.
unacceptable_sentence: The diagram laid out the footnote.
pair_rationale: Both objects are document/information nouns; diagram can present a process clearly, not normally a footnote.
critical_note: Present/organize-information sense is strong, but literal arrangement sense must be avoided.

acceptable_sentence: The students worked out the equation.
unacceptable_sentence: The students worked out the pencil.
pair_rationale: Both objects are classroom-domain nouns; equation is a problem that can be solved, whereas pencil is not.
critical_note: Solution/calculation sense is strong if both conditions involve problems or quantities.
